title: Exercises: Classical-Quantum System Architecture Design Patterns

exercises.title: Implement a Multi-Backend Adapter
exercises.description: Create a complete backend adapter system that supports multiple quantum providers with a unified interface.
tasks: Implement the base QuantumBackendAdapter abstract class with submit_circuit, get_result, and get_status methods
Create adapters for at least two providers: SimulatorAdapter and IBMAdapter
Implement a BackendAdapterFactory that creates the appropriate adapter based on configuration
Add connection testing and health check methods to each adapter
Implement proper error handling for backend-specific failures
exercises.expectedOutput: A working adapter system that can switch between simulator and IBM backends transparently
hints: Start with the SimulatorAdapter as it's easier to test locally
Use a dictionary to store job information in-memory for the simulator
Consider adding connection retry logic for the IBM adapter
The factory should validate configuration before creating adapters

testCases.description: Submit and retrieve job from simulator
testCases.expectedBehavior: Returns job ID and can retrieve results

exercises.title: Build a Variational Workflow Orchestrator
exercises.description: Implement a complete variational algorithm workflow orchestrator that coordinates parameter optimization with quantum circuit execution.
tasks: Create a VariationalWorkflow class that manages the optimization loop state
Implement a callback system for monitoring optimization progress
Add support for multiple optimization strategies (COBYLA, SPSA)
Implement early stopping based on convergence criteria
Create a comprehensive result object with convergence history
exercises.starterCode:
from typing import Callable, List, Tuple, Optional, Dict, Any
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
import numpy as np
from abc import ABC, abstractmethod

@dataclass
class OptimizationConfig:
    max_iterations: int = 100
    convergence_threshold: float = 1e-6
    shots: int = 1024
    early_stopping_patience: int = 10

@dataclass
class OptimizationResult:
    optimal_params: np.ndarray
    optimal_value: float
    iterations: int
    evaluations: int
    convergence_history: List[float]
    converged: bool
    final_state: str

class ProgressCallback:
    """Callback for optimization progress."""
    
    def on_iteration(self, iteration: int, params: np.ndarray, value: float):
        pass
    
    def on_evaluation(self, params: np.ndarray, value: float):
        pass
    
    def on_complete(self, result: OptimizationResult):
        pass

class VariationalWorkflow:
    """Orchestrates variational quantum-classical optimization."""
    
    def __init__(
        self,
        config: OptimizationConfig,
        circuit_executor: Callable[[np.ndarray], Dict[str, int]],
        cost_calculator: Callable[[Dict[str, int]], float]
    ):
        self.config = config
        self.circuit_executor = circuit_executor
        self.cost_calculator = cost_calculator
        self.callbacks: List[ProgressCallback] = []
        # TODO: Initialize state tracking
    
    def add_callback(self, callback: ProgressCallback):
        """Add progress callback."""
        self.callbacks.append(callback)
    
    def run(self, initial_params: np.ndarray, optimizer: str = "COBYLA") -> OptimizationResult:
        """Execute the variational optimization workflow."""

        # - Progress callbacks
        # - Convergence checking
        # - Early stopping
        pass
    
    def _check_convergence(self) -> bool:
        """Check if optimization has converged."""

        pass
    
    def _should_early_stop(self) -> bool:

exercises.expectedOutput: An orchestrator that runs VQE-style optimization with callbacks and early stopping
hints: Track both iteration count (optimizer steps) and evaluation count (circuit runs)
Store the best value seen to implement early stopping
Use variance of recent values to detect convergence
The callback should return True to stop optimization early

exercises.title: Design a Quantum Facade
exercises.description: Create a Facade pattern implementation that simplifies access to quantum computing operations for end users.
tasks: Implement a QuantumFacade class with simple high-level methods
Hide complexity of circuit creation, transpilation, and execution
Add configuration options for error mitigation and shots
Implement result interpretation and formatting
Add support for common algorithms: Bell state, GHZ state, VQE
exercises.starterCode:

exercises.expectedOutput: A simplified API that hides quantum complexity from end users
hints: The Facade should handle all circuit construction internally
Return rich result objects that include both raw data and interpretations
Include timing and metadata for debugging purposes
Consider caching circuits that are used repeatedly

exercises.title: Implement Optimizer Strategy Pattern
exercises.description: Create a complete Strategy pattern implementation for classical optimizers used in variational algorithms.
tasks: Define the OptimizationStrategy abstract base class
Implement at least three strategies: GradientDescent, COBYLA, and SPSA
Create an OptimizerContext that can switch strategies at runtime
Add hyperparameter configuration for each strategy
Implement a strategy selection helper based on problem characteristics
exercises.expectedOutput: Flexible optimizer selection system with runtime strategy switching
hints: SPSA is best for noisy quantum objectives
COBYLA works well for small to medium parameter counts
Gradient descent needs finite-difference gradients for quantum circuits
Include iteration history in metadata for analysis
